meta_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity project template to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity Template to Solve Debugging
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn how to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to debug faster and ship professional SaaS apps for clients.
content.answer_block: The Antigravity project template eliminates debugging bottlenecks by enforcing strict architectural patterns and automated error-handling hooks. By integrating Claude Code, you can automate root-cause analysis, allowing you to bypass manual trace-reading and resolve complex logic errors in minutes rather than hours, ensuring your client projects remain on schedule.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking. Your client is expecting a demo in four hours, but your terminal is bleeding red errors. You’ve spent the last sixty minutes chasing a ghost in the stack trace, and the frustration is mounting. This isn't just a bug; it's a productivity killer that threatens your reputation as a high-speed freelancer.

But what if you didn't have to hunt for bugs manually? The Antigravity project template changes the game by standardizing your codebase, making it inherently readable to AI agents. By pairing this structure with Claude Code, you transform from a frantic debugger into a systems architect who ships clean, verified code every time.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Workspace
steps.description: Standardizing your project structure is the first step to debugging success. Antigravity ensures your files are organized in a way that AI models can traverse without hallucinating.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project structure using the Antigravity standard template, ensuring all error logs are routed to a centralized /logs directory and middleware is configured for observability.

steps.title: Inject Debugging Observability
steps.description: Before you can fix a bug, you must see it. Use Antigravity to inject structured logging into your service layers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add structured logging and error interception wrappers to all API controllers to capture stack traces and input parameters for every failed request.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add logging middleware to src/controllers'

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Point
steps.description: Instead of reading the entire codebase, use Claude Code to perform a targeted diagnostic scan on the failing module.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current error logs in /logs and identify the specific function responsible for the runtime crash.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Analyze logs in /logs and identify the root cause of the current runtime error'

steps.title: Automated Remediation
steps.description: Once the issue is identified, leverage AI to refactor the broken code while maintaining the project's architectural integrity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the identified faulty function to include defensive programming and input validation, following the Antigravity best practices.

steps.title: Regression Testing and Verification
steps.description: Never ship a fix without verifying it doesn't break existing features. Use the template's built-in testing suite.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate unit tests for the newly refactored code and run the full test suite to ensure zero regressions.

pro_tips: Always set your logs to JSON format; it makes parsing via Claude Code significantly faster and more accurate.
Use a 'debug-only' branch in your Git workflow so you can revert to a stable state instantly if the AI refactor causes edge-case failures.
Pre-emptively add 'Health Check' endpoints to your Antigravity template to catch configuration bugs before they hit production.
When asking Claude to debug, always provide the specific error code AND the last three successful interactions to provide context.
Keep your project modules small; Antigravity excels when logic is decoupled, making it easier for AI to isolate bugs within specific files.
resources: Best Practices for AI Engineering: https://ai-builder.community/handbook
content.next_steps: After completing the debug cycle, deploy your project to a staging environment and run a smoke test to ensure all user flows remain intact.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start scaling your freelance business with AI-driven workflows that keep you ahead of the competition. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
